channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: 🇨🇳 Trump and Xi conclude Beijing summit with new trade deals, Iran tensions dominate agenda
item.description: US President Trump completed high-stakes talks with Chinese leader Xi Jinping in Beijing, announcing trade agreements while Xi warned on Taiwan. Both leaders discussed Iran and Strait of Hormuz tensions, with China wanting the waterway open without restrictions.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🛢️ China wants Strait of Hormuz open without restrictions, USTR Greer confirms
item.description: U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer told Bloomberg that China wants the Strait of Hormuz reopened without curbs or tolls, and that Washington is confident Beijing will limit material support for Iran amid ongoing conflict.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 💰 Oil prices rise as Trump signals impatience with Iran over Hormuz attacks
item.description: Crude oil gained over 1% as President Trump said he would not be much more patient with Iran, with concerns persisting over ship attacks and seizures despite Tehran claiming 30 vessels had passed through the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇷🇺 Russia ramps up energy exports to cushion global markets from Iran war fallout
item.description: Despite sanctions, Russia is playing a key role cushioning global commodity markets from the Iran war impact, increasing energy exports to stabilize markets amid Middle East supply disruptions.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇫🇷 Macron acknowledges France outpaced in Africa by China, Turkey and US
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron acknowledged that France has lost competitive ground in Africa to China, Turkey and the United States, as European influence on the continent continues to decline.
item.dc:creator: Anadolu

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 Trump warns patience with Iran running thin as diplomacy pressure mounts
item.description: President Donald Trump said he would not be much more patient with Iran as he urged Tehran to reach a deal with Washington, suggesting the focus on enriched uranium retrieval was more about perception than security.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇸🇧🇨🇳 Solomon Islands elects former China critic Matthew Wale as prime minister
item.description: Parliament elected opposition leader Matthew Wale as prime minister after incumbent Jeremiah Manele was ousted in a no-confidence vote, potentially shifting the Pacific nation's stance on China relations.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇨🇳🛢️ China wants Strait of Hormuz open without restrictions, USTR Greer says
item.description: U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer told Bloomberg News that China wants the Strait of Hormuz reopened without curbs or tolls, and expressed confidence Beijing would limit material support for Iran.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇳⚖️ US Justice Department set to drop criminal fraud case against India's Gautam Adani
item.description: The U.S. Justice Department is close to dropping criminal fraud charges against Indian billionaire Gautam Adani, who has promised to invest $10 billion in the U.S. economy, according to sources familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🏛️💰 IMF welcomes constructive US-China dialogue as tensions ease
item.description: The International Monetary Fund said reduced tension and uncertainty between the world's two largest economies would benefit the global economy, as Trump and Xi held their first summit meeting.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇱🇻 Latvian Prime Minister Resigns Over Ukrainian Drone Incident Handling
item.description: Prime Minister Evika Silina resigned, triggering coalition government collapse months before October elections, amid controversy over handling of Ukrainian drone incidents.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 US Expects Double-Digit Billions in Chinese Farm Purchases After Trump-Xi Summit
item.description: US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer announced expectations of major Chinese agricultural purchases following the Beijing summit, signaling potential trade breakthrough.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇺🇦💰 IMF Team to Visit Ukraine for $8.1 Billion Loan Program Review
item.description: International Monetary Fund staff will visit Ukraine in coming weeks to review progress on economic reforms and tax base expansion under the major loan program.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇷🇺💥 Gazprom Astrakhan Gas Plant Halts Output After Drone Attack
item.description: Russia's Gazprom gas processing plant in Astrakhan halted motor fuel production following a May 13 drone attack that caused a fire at the facility.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇹🇼🇺🇸 Taiwan Thanks US for Support Ahead of Further Trump-Xi Talks
item.description: Taiwan expressed gratitude to the US for reaffirming support and commitment to peace and stability, as Trump's China visit continues with Taiwan a key topic.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇮🇱⚔️ Israel Pushing for Return to Iran War, Weighing Options With US
item.description: Israeli military officials discussed renewed operations, maritime blockade and other scenarios for resuming conflict with Iran, according to an Israeli broadcaster report.
item.dc:creator: Anadolu Agency

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 Trump Blames Biden for US Decline After Xi Comments on Thucydides Trap
item.description: President Trump invoked his predecessor to explain Chinese President Xi Jinping comments suggesting the US is a declining nation, as the two leaders held high-stakes talks in Beijing.
item.dc:creator: The Hill

item.title: 🇺🇦 Putin Escalating War, Not Seeking End, Says German Chancellor Candidate Merz
item.description: German politician Friedrich Merz condemned Russia massive aerial attacks on Ukraine, saying Putin is escalating rather than seeking peace. Kyiv faced its third straight day of Russian strikes with apartment buildings flattened.
item.dc:creator: The Guardian

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russia Losing Ground in Ukraine for First Time Since Kursk Incursion
item.description: Ukraine liberated more territory than Russia seized last month, marking Moscows first net loss of territory since Ukraines 2024 Kursk operation. The shift comes as Russia launched its largest aerial assault of the war.
item.dc:creator: CNN

item.title: 🇸🇦 Saudi Arabia and UAE Carried Out Secret Attacks Inside Iran
item.description: US officials revealed that Gulf Arab states conducted covert operations in Iran, seeking to deter Iranian aggression after the war exposed limits of American security guarantees. The attacks reflect regional unease about Iranian military capabilities.
item.dc:creator: New York Times

item.title: 🇨🇳🇺🇸 China halts US beef export licenses hours after Trump-Xi summit approval
item.description: Chinese customs appeared to halt export clearances for hundreds of US beef plants just hours after Reuters reported the long-awaited licenses had been approved during the Trump-Xi summit in Beijing.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇯🇵 Japan wholesale inflation hits 3-year high as Iran war drives energy prices
item.description: Japanese wholesale inflation accelerated at the fastest pace in three years in April as the Iran conflict boosted oil and chemical prices, strengthening the case for a central bank rate hike as soon as June.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇲🇦 Morocco adds B to budget to cushion Middle East conflict impact
item.description: The Moroccan government plans to add 20 billion dirhams to its 2026 budget to finance measures cushioning the impact of the Middle East conflict on the domestic market.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇮🇶 Iraq seeks IMF and World Bank financial assistance amid Iran war fallout
item.description: Iraqi officials have approached the International Monetary Fund about securing financial assistance as a result of the Middle East conflict, according to a source close to the IMF.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇺🇸🇵🇱 US cancels planned deployment of 4,000 troops to Poland
item.description: Pentagon scraps temporary troop deployment to Poland, surprising lawmakers and raising questions about US commitment to European defense amid Iran war tensions.
item.dc:creator: The Star
